briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for June 21, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis Frontline: Domestic and International Extreme Weather and Warnings
sections.content: Signs of crisis due to climate change are becoming increasingly evident both domestically and internationally. Analysis indicating that the unusual high temperatures on the East Coast and the June heatwave are rooted in the ocean demonstrates the direct impact on our lives, alongside changes in marine ecosystems [3, 10]. Internationally, climate change is exacerbating complex crises by increasing soybean yields but deteriorating quality, creating a vicious cycle in road systems [34, 37], and complicating foodborne diseases when combined with chemical pollutants and antibiotic resistance [44]. The Asian Development Bank (ADB) has warned that climate change is triggering a silent mental health crisis across the Asia-Pacific region [45], and in Greece, large-scale personnel and equipment have been deployed to suppress wildfires [46], demonstrating the immediate impacts of the climate crisis globally.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts to achieve carbon neutrality are accelerating. Daegu City has expanded its carbon neutrality governance and strengthened citizen participation in the Climate Crisis Response Committee [11], while Gwangmyeong City youth are practicing carbon neutrality through 'Jupking' activities [12]. Jeonnam Province is also fully embarking on carbon neutrality practices [19], and in the livestock sector, arguments are being made to transform reduction into a 'value' [20], indicating that concrete measures for carbon neutrality are being sought in various fields. Furthermore, policies that encourage citizen participation and expand practical spaces are actively being promoted, including exploring ways to utilize eco-friendly railways [16], expanding wood culture experience centers [17, 18], and providing local currency through the pilot operation of 'Carbon Moa Tantane' [15]. Asan City Facilities Management Corporation and Hoseo University's Anchor Project Group have signed a business agreement for carbon neutrality [21, 25], strengthening community-level cooperation.

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Response Strategies and Innovations
sections.content: The international community is also continuing to develop strategies and innovative initiatives to address climate change. Europe is easing regulations on gene-edited crops in response to climate change, exploring new pathways for food security [1]. Meanwhile, the recovery of the ozone layer is presented as a successful case of international cooperation, offering important lessons for Korea's carbon neutrality strategy [9, 26]. Discussions and participation in climate change are being encouraged in various ways, including the climate change visualization campaign #ShowYourStripes [33], zero-waste habit education [39], and donations [42] [35]. Furthermore, creative and practical solutions to combat climate change are being proposed globally, such as mitigation measures through a single meal [36], the use of white roofs [38], the role of fungi [40], the introduction of smart automation technology [41], and community efforts through boat races [43].

sections.title: 📰 Opportunities and Challenges of Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy still presents both numerous opportunities and challenges. While the market potential is immense, with multi-trillion dollar investment opportunities arising in the renewable energy sector and 'big players worth 200 trillion won' identifying Korea as an investment destination [32], there are also numerous challenges to address, such as renewable energy insurance prices decreasing in 2026 while technological barriers are rising [28]. Furthermore, concerns have been raised that the rapid expansion of solar power could inadvertently foster an 'Assembly Korea' dependent on China [30]. Domestically, the Jecheon City Foundation for Fostering Disabled Talent is exploring new avenues by signing a business agreement for cooperation in the renewable energy sector [29], and the role of hydropower and pumped-hydro storage is being highlighted as crucial for power stability in the era of renewable energy [31]. However, the case of US Democratic stronghold states 'waving the white flag' on climate goals despite the availability of the cheapest solar power [4] suggests that the renewable energy transition is a complex political and social challenge, extending beyond mere technical issues.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has clearly highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, the multifaceted domestic and international efforts to combat it, and the challenges of the renewable energy transition. Through continued attention and cooperation, we must strive to build a more sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.
